cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /vibe. The develop stage just finished. TaskList shows the epic has 4 children: 3 completed, 1 still in_progress (worker crashed mid-implementation). git diff --stat shows changes from all 4 tasks including partial work from the crashed worker.

Demonstrate what you do next.

expectations.description: Checks child task statuses and recognizes that not all children completed — does not assume develop succeeded because it returned
Does NOT proceed to the commit stage when a child task is still in_progress with partial work in the tree
Reports which tasks succeeded and which failed, and suggests recovery options (e.g., /vibe --continue or /develop to retry)

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /vibe 'add user avatars'. Branch created, scope completed successfully (vibe_stage='scope', scope task approved with metadata.design populated). You invoke Skill('develop') but it errors out completely — the skill call fails with no progress made.

Demonstrate error handling.

expectations.description: Does NOT update vibe_stage to 'develop' — it stays at 'scope' because the develop stage completely failed with no progress
Leaves the vibe tracker task in_progress (not completed or failed) so --continue can find and resume it
Reports which stages completed vs failed and suggests recovery: /vibe --continue or /develop to retry the failed stage directly

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /vibe 'add webhook retry with exponential backoff'. You have created the branch and are now about to invoke the Scope stage.

Show the exact Skill() call you make for the scope stage. Then explain what scope does differently when invoked this way vs when the user invokes /scope directly.

expectations.description: The Skill call includes '--auto-approve' flag: Skill('scope', args='<prompt> --no-develop --auto-approve') — this skips user review and goes straight to approval

Explains that --auto-approve causes scope to skip BOTH spec review and plan review — the spec (what to build) and plan (how to build it) are both auto-approved instead of stopping for user feedback at each gate
Scope produces a spec (metadata.spec) and implementation plan (metadata.design) with status_detail='approved' — it does NOT create epics or tasks (develop handles that)

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /vibe 'add auth passthrough for tests'. You just invoked Skill('start', args='luan/auth-passthrough') and it returned successfully: branch created, and /start suggested 'suggest /scope or /develop'. git branch --show-current confirms you're on the new branch.

What do you do immediately after updating vibe_stage to 'branch'?

expectations.description: Immediately invokes Skill('scope', args='<prompt> --no-develop --auto-approve') — does NOT pause, suggest next steps, output branch results, or wait for user input
Outputs the stage announcement (e.g., '[2/4] Scope') as text BEFORE invoking Skill('scope')
Does NOT echo or act on /start's 'suggest /scope or /develop' output — treats it as noise and proceeds with the pipeline

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /vibe 'add dark mode toggle'. Branch created (vibe_stage='branch'). You just invoked Skill('scope', args='add dark mode toggle --no-develop --auto-approve') and it returned successfully. TaskList shows a scope task with status_detail='approved' and metadata.design populated. --dry-run was NOT passed.

What do you do immediately after updating vibe_stage to 'scope'?

expectations.description: Immediately invokes Skill('develop') — does NOT pause, output a scope summary, ask the user what to do next, or wait for user input
Outputs the stage announcement (e.g., '[3/6] Develop') as text BEFORE invoking Skill('develop')
Does NOT output scope results, phase breakdowns, key files, or any summary of what scope produced — proceeds directly to develop

cases.prompt:
You are Claude running /vibe 'add rate limiting middleware'. All prior stages completed (branch, scope, develop). vibe_stage='develop'. All epic children show status='completed'. git diff --stat shows changes across 5 files.

What do you do immediately after updating vibe_stage to 'develop'?

expectations.description: Immediately invokes Skill('simplify') — does NOT skip to review or commit
After simplify completes, invokes Skill('review') before proceeding to commit
Does NOT pause, summarize develop results, or wait for user input between develop → simplify → review → commit

cases.prompt:
You are Claude about to run /vibe 'add search feature'. No flags passed. You're on the main branch.

List all stages in order with their stage numbers [N/M].

expectations.description: M = 6 for the full pipeline: branch, scope, develop, simplify, review, commit
Stages are ordered: [1/6] Branch, [2/6] Scope, [3/6] Develop, [4/6] Simplify, [5/6] Review, [6/6] Commit
